title: The Photo in Bia's Phone
description: Bia shows the class a family photo. Meet her mother, father, brother, and grandmother — and the possessive adjectives along the way.
summary_pt_br: A Bia mostra a foto da família à turma. Aprenda os membros da família e os possessivos (my/your/his/her) — incluindo a armadilha do 'parents' (apenas pai e mãe, não parentes em geral).

scenes.subtitle_en: A photo passed around the class
scenes.subtitle_pt: Uma foto que circula pela sala

scenes.text_en:
Bia pulled out her phone and pressed 'show'.
'Meet my family,' she said in a glow.
'My mom is a nurse. My dad runs a store.
My brother is TWO — plus Grandma next door.'
scenes.text_pt:
A Bia puxou o celular e apertou 'mostrar'.
'Conheçam minha família,' disse a brilhar.
'Minha mãe é enfermeira. Meu pai tem um mercado.
Meu irmão tem DOIS anos — e a avó, ali do lado.'

scenes.text_en:
My mother's tall. My father's short.
My brother's two — the youngest sort.
My grandma reads. My grandpa sings.
My family's a funny little thing.
scenes.text_pt:
Minha mãe é alta. Meu pai, baixinho.
Meu irmão tem dois — o mais novinho.
Vovó adora ler. Vovô canta a voz.
Minha família é um grudinho veloz.

scenes.title: Possessives match the OWNER, not the thing
scenes.body_en: The word for 'dele' or 'dela' depends on WHO owns, not WHAT is owned. Paul's sister = HIS sister (Paul is male). Anna's brother = HER brother (Anna is female). In Portuguese 'sua' covers both — in English you must pick.
scenes.body_pt: Em inglês, o possessivo concorda com o DONO, não com o objeto. Paul é homem → HIS sister (a irmã DELE). Anna é mulher → HER brother (o irmão DELA). Em português 'sua' cobre tudo — em inglês você escolhe.

examples.en: Rafa is tall. His truck is red.
examples.pt: O Rafa é alto. A caminhonete DELE é vermelha.

examples.en: Maya is smart. Her pencil is sharp.
examples.pt: A Maya é esperta. O lápis DELA é apontado.

exercise.question: Complete: 'Paul is tall. ___ sister is short.'
exercise.explanation: Paul is male → HIS. (The sister is female, but what matters is PAUL.)
exercise.hint_pt_br: Paul é homem, então o possessivo é HIS — não importa o gênero da irmã.

exercise.explanation: 'Cousin' works for both boys and girls — English doesn't mark the gender there.
exercise.hint_pt_br: 'Cousin' serve para primo E prima. Já 'nephew' (sobrinho) e 'niece' (sobrinha) são separados.

scenes.location_pt: Intervalo da aula, passando o celular

turns.en: Yes, HE is my brother. HE is two years old.
turns.pt: Sim, ELE é meu irmão. ELE tem dois anos.

turns.en: And who is the lady in blue?
turns.pt: E quem é a senhora de azul?

turns.en: SHE is my grandma. HER hair is white.
turns.pt: ELA é minha avó. O cabelo DELA é branco.

exercise.question: 'My parents' in English means…
exercise.explanation: FALSE FRIEND alert. 'Parents' = only mom + dad. 'Relatives' is the broader word for the Portuguese 'parentes'.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Falso amigo! 'Parents' NÃO é 'parentes' — é só mãe e pai. Parentes em geral = 'relatives'.

exercise.question: Two of my friends come to class. 'I have two ___.' (friend → plural)
exercise.explanation: Regular plural: just add -s. Friend → friends.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Plural regular: só adicione -s. Friend → friends.

scenes.text_en:
Dona Lúcia smiled at the photo and sighed.
'My husband is gone, but my love never died.
HIS letters are mine. HIS words are still here.
Family,' she said, 'stays always near.'
scenes.text_pt:
Dona Lúcia olhou a foto e suspirou suave.
'Meu marido se foi, mas o amor não se cala.
AS cartas DELE são minhas. AS palavras, presentes.
Família,' disse ela, 'fica sempre com a gente.'

scenes.title: Showing family photos
scenes.pt_summary: Vocabulário de família — mother, father, brother, sister.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Hey, look at this photo. This is my family.
turns.pt: Ei, olha essa foto. Essa é minha família.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Who is the man on the left?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: That is my father. He is a lawyer.
turns.pt: É meu pai. Ele é advogado.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: And the woman in the middle?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: That is my mother. She is a doctor.
turns.pt: É minha mãe. Ela é médica.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: You have a big family. How many siblings?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Two — one brother and one sister.
turns.pt: Dois — um irmão e uma irmã.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: She is twelve. She loves to swim.

scenes.title: Listening story — The Photo in Bia's Phone
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça a situação com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que você entendeu — resumo, detalhes, seus pensamentos. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Write in English what you heard. A short summary is fine. Mention who spoke, what they said, and what you think about the situation.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que você ouviu. Pode ser um resumo curto. Diga quem falou, o que disseram e o que você achou.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In this story, we meet Bia and a student. Bia shows the class a family photo. Meet her mother, father, brother, and grandmother — and the possessive adjectives along the way. Listen carefully and think about what each person says.

paragraphs.speaker: Bia
paragraphs.en: Hey, look at this photo. This is my family.

paragraphs.speaker: The student
paragraphs.en: Who is the man on the left?

paragraphs.speaker: Bia
paragraphs.en: That is my father. He is a lawyer.

paragraphs.speaker: The student
paragraphs.en: And the woman in the middle?

paragraphs.speaker: Bia
paragraphs.en: That is my mother. She is a doctor.

paragraphs.speaker: The student
paragraphs.en: You have a big family. How many siblings?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: That is the end of our short scene with Bia. Think about what you heard and how the conversation felt.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "The Photo in Bia's Phone". Bia shows the class a family photo. Meet her mother, father, brother, and grandmother — and the possessive adjectives along the way. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave é a prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus próprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "The Photo in Bia's Phone". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'The Photo in Bia's Phone'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o título: "The Photo in Bia's Phone".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para você estudar mais. Salve os links e volte sempre.

sources.label: VOA Learning English
sources.pt_hint: Notícias em inglês lento — ótimo para ouvido iniciante.

sources.label: English Grammar In Use (preview)
